evals.prompt: I have an Astro website at /home/z/my-project/site with a shared layout in src/layouts/Base.astro, a Navbar component, and pages for Home and About. I need to add a new /pricing page. Make sure it looks consistent with the existing pages.
evals.expected_output: A new pricing page that imports and wraps content in Base.astro (not its own HTML shell), registers as a route via the file-based routing convention, adds a 'Pricing' link to the shared Navbar component, passes title and description to the layout, uses the same Tailwind/style conventions as existing pages, and includes no duplicated <html>/<head>/<body>/<nav>/<footer> markup.
expectations: The new page file imports and wraps content in the existing Base.astro layout
The page file does NOT contain <html>, <head>, <body>, <nav>, or <footer> tags
A 'Pricing' link is added to the shared Navbar component (not inside the page file)
The page passes title and description props to the layout
Internal links use relative paths or the same base URL utility as existing pages
The page uses the same CSS/styling approach (Tailwind, scoped styles, etc.) as existing pages
The page reflows correctly at mobile/tablet/desktop breakpoints

evals.prompt: Review this Next.js project at /home/z/my-project/next-site for architectural consistency. It has 8 pages and I suspect the LLM that built some of them copied the shell into individual page files. Check for layout fragmentation, navigation drift, and style inconsistencies. Write findings to /home/z/my-project/arch-review.md.
evals.expected_output: A review document that identifies: which pages duplicate the shell (have their own <html>/<head>/<body> or nav/footer markup instead of using the shared layout), whether navigation links are consistent across pages, whether meta tags are present and consistent, whether styles follow a single method, and whether internal links use relative paths. Each finding includes the specific file, line, and remediation step.
expectations: Identifies any page files that contain shell markup (<html>, <head>, <body>, <nav>, <footer>)
Identifies navigation links that are duplicated or missing across pages
Identifies pages with missing or inconsistent meta tags (title, description, OG)
Identifies style method inconsistencies (e.g., mixing Tailwind and inline styles)
Identifies hardcoded absolute URLs in internal links
Identifies trailing-slash inconsistencies
Provides specific remediation steps for each finding (not just 'fix it')

evals.prompt: Our Vue/Nuxt project at /home/z/my-project/nuxt-app has a layout in layouts/default.vue with a sidebar and footer. I need to add three new pages: /reports, /analytics, /settings. These should all share the same layout. Also make sure the sidebar gets updated with links to the new pages.
evals.expected_output: Three new page files in pages/ that use <NuxtLayout> or are automatically wrapped by the default layout. Each page contains only its unique content. The sidebar component (or the layout's navigation data) is updated with links to /reports, /analytics, and /settings. Each page provides title/description via useHead() or useSeoMeta(). No page contains duplicated shell markup.
expectations: Each new page file is placed in the correct Nuxt pages/ directory for file-based routing
No page file contains <html>, <head>, <body>, or the sidebar/footer component markup
The sidebar navigation is updated in ONE place (the shared sidebar component or nav data file)
Each page provides meta values (title, description) using Nuxt's head management
The pages use the same styling conventions as existing pages
Internal links between the three new pages and existing pages use relative paths or <NuxtLink>
